proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e == 0`.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 0`.
The apparent BRAF dependency is enriched in `lineage == "skin"` cell lines, which should have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than other lineages.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_BRAF by braf_v600e status.
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e == 1` lines had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.8047 versus -0.2495 in non-mutant lines (n=5574 vs 44426), a difference of -0.5552.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_EGFR by egfr_amplification status.
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6010 versus -0.2524 in non-amplified lines (n=3880 vs 46120), a difference of -0.3486.

analyses.method: Single-predicate heterogeneity screen by lineage.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "skin"` had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.4721 versus -0.2938 in other lineages (n=4935), a difference of -0.1783.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 0`.
Cell lines with `kras_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation == 0`.
`lineage == "pancreatic"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than non-pancreatic cell lines.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_ERBB2 by erbb2_amplification status.
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.6856 versus -0.2535 in non-amplified lines (n=4890 vs 45110), a difference of -0.4321.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KRAS by kras_mutation status.
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.5594 versus -0.2520 in wild-type lines (n=11842 vs 38158), a difference of -0.3075.

analyses.method: Single-predicate heterogeneity screen by lineage.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "pancreatic"` had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.4260 versus -0.3133 in other lineages (n=5102), a difference of -0.1127.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss == 0`.
The `dependency_RIT1` effect is stronger in the subgroup with `lineage == "brain"` and `nf1_loss == 1` than in all other cell lines.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RIT1 by nf1_loss status.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.3527 versus -0.2572 in other lines (n=4560 vs 45440), a difference of -0.0955.

analyses.method: Pair-predicate heterogeneity screen.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "brain"` and `nf1_loss == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.4047 versus -0.2650 in all other lines (n=329), a difference of -0.1397.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage == "colorectal"` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation == 0`.
Cell lines in the top quartile of `wnt_activity_score` (`wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than the remaining cell lines.

analyses.method: Single-predicate heterogeneity screen.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "colorectal"` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 versus -0.2524 in other lineages (n=5816), a difference of -0.4680.

analyses.method: Single-predicate heterogeneity screen.
analyses.result_summary: `apc_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.5447 versus -0.2526 in `apc_mutation == 0` lines (n=9285), a difference of -0.2921.

analyses.method: Top-quartile threshold comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.4694 versus -0.2526 in lower-WNT lines (n=12508), a difference of -0.2168.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup is the complete subgroup with `lineage == "colorectal"` and `apc_mutation == 1`, which has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
A related `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup with `lineage == "colorectal"` and `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
High aneuploidy alone (`aneuploidy_score` in the top quartile) makes `dependency_KIF18A` scores more negative than in other cell lines.

analyses.method: Pair-predicate subgroup comparison from the systematic dependency-heterogeneity search.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "colorectal"` and `apc_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.9425 versus -0.2527 in all other lines (n=3925), a difference of -0.6899.

analyses.method: Pair-predicate subgroup comparison from the systematic dependency-heterogeneity search.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "colorectal"` and `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.9288 versus -0.2527 in all other lines (n=4006), a difference of -0.6761.

analyses.method: Top-quartile threshold comparison.
analyses.result_summary: Top-quartile `aneuploidy_score` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.3078 versus -0.3065 in other lines (n=12521), a difference of only -0.0013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing lines.
`lineage == "colorectal"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
The subgroup with `lineage_subtype == "luminal"` and `stk11_loss == 1` has more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.method: Top-quartile threshold comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2816 versus -0.2447 in slower-growing lines (n=12577), a difference of -0.0369.

analyses.method: Lineage subgroup comparison.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "colorectal"` lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2576 versus -0.2535 in other lineages (n=5816), a difference of -0.0040.

analyses.method: Pair-predicate subgroup comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage_subtype == "luminal"` and `stk11_loss == 1` lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.3078 versus -0.2537 in other lines (n=243), a difference of -0.0541; this was nominally significant but much smaller and less robust than the proliferation-related signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` (`rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669`) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than lower-MYC cell lines.
The subgroup with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` has more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines in the top quartile of `rnaseq_SLFN11_log2_tpm` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than lower-SLFN11 cell lines.

analyses.method: Top-quartile threshold comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3551 versus -0.3265 in lower-MYC lines (n=12501), a difference of -0.0286.

analyses.method: Pair-predicate subgroup comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3892 versus -0.3297 in other lines (n=3320), a difference of -0.0595.

analyses.method: Top-quartile threshold comparison.
analyses.result_summary: Top-quartile `rnaseq_SLFN11_log2_tpm` lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3331 versus -0.3338 in lower-SLFN11 lines (n=12503), a difference of +0.0007.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines in the top quartile of `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (`growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing lines.
The subgroup with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `copy_number_9p21 <= -0.497` has more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than all other cell lines.
Low `copy_number_9p21` alone (`copy_number_9p21 <= -0.497`) makes `dependency_POLR2A` scores more negative than in other cell lines.

analyses.method: Top-quartile threshold comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4972 versus -0.3928 in slower-growing lines (n=12577), a difference of -0.1043.

analyses.method: Pair-predicate subgroup comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` and `copy_number_9p21 <= -0.497` lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.5098 versus -0.4131 in other lines (n=3111), a difference of -0.0967.

analyses.method: Bottom-quartile threshold comparison.
analyses.result_summary: `copy_number_9p21 <= -0.497` lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4197 versus -0.4189 in other lines (n=12510), a difference of -0.0009.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `myc_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `myc_amplification == 0`.
Cell lines in the top quartile of `copy_number_8q24` (`copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428`) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than other cell lines.
The subgroup with `myc_amplification == 1` and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` has more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RPL11 by myc_amplification status.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3614 versus -0.2523 in non-amplified lines (n=8055 vs 41945), a difference of -0.1091.

analyses.method: Top-quartile threshold comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3440 versus -0.2452 in lower-8q24 lines (n=12501), a difference of -0.0988.

analyses.method: Pair-predicate subgroup comparison from the systematic screen.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification == 1` and `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3936 versus -0.2632 in other lines (n=2557), a difference of -0.1303.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic single-feature and pair-feature dependency-heterogeneity search should recover the strongest subgroup for each dependency outcome, including canonical driver dependencies and non-canonical subgroups.
For `dependency_KIF18A`, the best complete subgroup from the systematic search is `lineage == "colorectal"` and `apc_mutation == 1`, with more negative scores than all other cell lines.

For housekeeping-like outcomes `dependency_TMED10`, `dependency_DHX9`, and `dependency_POLR2A`, the best-supported heterogeneity is mainly related to high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, with only weak molecular subgroup effects.

analyses.method: Systematic screen of all dependency outcomes using lineage, lineage_subtype, culture_type, screen_batch, all binary molecular features, top and bottom quartiles of all continuous features, and all eligible pairwise predicate intersections with at least 100 cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: Best single-predicate effects were: `dependency_BRAF` with `braf_v600e == 1` (-0.5552), `dependency_EGFR` with `egfr_amplification == 1` (-0.3486), `dependency_ERBB2` with `erbb2_amplification == 1` (-0.4321), `dependency_KRAS` with `kras_mutation == 1` (-0.3075), `dependency_RIT1` with `nf1_loss == 1` (-0.0955), `dependency_KIF18A` with `lineage == "colorectal"` (-0.4680), `dependency_TMED10` with high growth rate (-0.0369), `dependency_DHX9` with high growth rate (-0.0371), `dependency_POLR2A` with high growth rate (-0.1043), and `dependency_RPL11` with `myc_amplification == 1` (-0.1091).

analyses.method: Best pairwise predicate from the systematic search for dependency_KIF18A.
analyses.result_summary: Among all screened pairwise subgroups, `lineage == "colorectal"` plus `apc_mutation == 1` produced the largest negative `dependency_KIF18A` shift: -0.9425 versus -0.2527 in all other lines, effect -0.6899.

analyses.method: Systematic search review for dependency_TMED10, dependency_DHX9, and dependency_POLR2A.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_TMED10` was most consistently associated with high growth rate (effect -0.0369); `dependency_DHX9` was stronger in high-growth/high-MYC lines (effect -0.0595); `dependency_POLR2A` was strongly associated with high growth rate (effect -0.1043), while low `copy_number_9p21` alone was not supported (effect -0.0009, p=0.811).
